zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud Hobridge於1213年出生在英國艾可時，她的父親William Hobrugge23歲，母親Agnes Picot19歲。她於大約1239年與William la Zouche結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud Hobridge于1213年出生在英国艾可时，她的父亲William Hobrugge23岁，母亲Agnes Picot19岁。她于大约1239年与William la Zouche结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Hobridge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Owbridge
meanings: 1 English: (i) locative name, possibly from Hull Bridge in Beverley (ER Yorks), which is recorded as Ulbrigg in about 1120 (in an MS dated 1398) and Hulbrig, Hulbryg from 1394 to 1543. The place takes its name from the River Hull + Old English brycg ‘bridge’. A late Middle English change of -ul- to -ol-, and then to -ou-, occurs in Owram (from the ER Yorks place-name Ulrome). Similarly, (H)ulbryg could have developed to (H)oubrig. (ii) locative name. Alternatively Owbridge might have derived from Howe Bridge in Malton (NR Yorks), which is recorded as ponte de Hou in the 12th and 13th centuries and as ponte de How in the 15th century ( ponte being the dative form of Latin pons ‘bridge’). The place-name derives from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + brycg. The post-medieval bearers cited here may alternatively belong with (2).

2 English: variant of Outbridge, an obsolete pronunciation of Oughtibridge , a WR Yorks name. Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorks Surnames notes that a man named German Owbridge or Outbridge of Meadow Lane is recorded in 1651 in the Leeds Parish Registers , and that in the 1672 Hearth Tax Henry Outbridg was registered in Market Weighton (ER Yorks), where Owbridge is also on record.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud Hobridge was born in 1213, in Essex, England, her father, William Hobrugge, was 23 and her mother, Agnes Picot, was 19. She married William la Zouche about 1239. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud Hobridge est née en 1213, à Essex, Angleterre, son père, William Hobrugge, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Agnes Picot, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé William la Zouche environ 1239. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud Hobridge 1213 in Essex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Hobrugge 23 und ihre Mutter Agnes Picot 19. Sie hat um 1239 William la Zouche geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Hobridge è nata il nel 1213 nel Essex, England, sua padre, William Hobrugge, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Agnes Picot, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato William la Zouche circa 1239. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud Hobridgeが1213年にイギリスエセックスで生まれた時，父William Hobruggeは23歳，母Agnes Picotは19歳であった。約1239年にWilliam la Zoucheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud Hobridge(이)가 1213년 영국 에세스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Hobrugge(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Picot(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 대략 1239년 William la Zouche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Hobridge nasceu em 1213, em Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Hobrugge, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Picot, tinha 19 anos. Ela casou-se com William la Zouche aproximadamente 1239. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud Hobridge родилась в 1213 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени William Hobrugge было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes Picot, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William la Zouche около 1239 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud Hobridge nació en 1213, en Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, William Hobrugge, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Agnes Picot, tenía 19 años. Se casó con William la Zouche aproximadamente 1239. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en su pueblo.
